name: DG sea acceptance per UN/NA identification number
purpose: Supports Cortex readiness checks for dangerous-goods cargo by identifying the hazardous material, hazard class, packing group, vessel stowage category, and vessel stowage/handling provision codes before sea booking.

sources.name: eCFR 49 CFR 172.101 - Hazardous Materials Table
sources.use: Authoritative column definitions and current regulatory reference; web source confirms Table purpose and vessel stowage column meanings.

sources.name: Marshall University Safety Office DOT Hazardous Materials Excel extract
sources.use: Structured extraction source used to populate the row dataset; legacy Excel source, not guaranteed current.

sources.name: eCFR 49 CFR 176.63 - Stowage locations
sources.use: Physical requirements for authorized stowage locations.

sources.name: eCFR 49 CFR 176.84 - Other stowage, handling and segregation requirements
sources.use: Reference for vessel stowage/handling provision codes in column 10B.

sources.name: IMO IMDG Code overview
sources.use: International maritime DG context and limitation: full IMDG Dangerous Goods List is not included.

known_limitations: Main row population uses a legacy structured Excel extract because a current eCFR machine-readable table extract was not available in this environment. Treat row values as R&D/reference and re-validate against current eCFR/PHMSA before production.
This is not a full IMDG Code Dangerous Goods List and does not replace certified dangerous goods review.
IMDG segregation groups are not fully derived; rows are flagged for manual review where provision codes suggest stowage/handling/segregation constraints.
Carrier-specific DG acceptance, terminal-specific DG acceptance, vessel Document of Compliance, and live local restrictions are out of scope.
Air quantity limitation columns are retained as source context but SEA-A readiness logic focuses on sea/vessel stowage.
quality_controls: Proper shipping name digit-start validation applied. Numeric-leading source names are normalized with a non-numeric prefix and the original is preserved in _raw to prevent the air-style column-shift parser bug.
Continuation rows for multi-packing-group entries inherited prior proper shipping name and identification number.
Rows without UN/NA identification numbers were excluded from main per-identification-number dataset and counted in coverage summary.
